generated_comment:
A: These boys in office keep saying those mother-sister type gaalis for everything.
B: Arre yaar, half the time it is just expression, not like they are literally talking about any woman.
A: Exactly, people are making everything into a gender issue now. Even saying a man is behaving like a woman is just a way of saying he is being weak, bas.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/chastity-based abuse as harmless expression and explicitly uses femininity as a synonym for weakness. It encodes the frame's 'it's just a word' defense and feminizing-as-insult structure.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 38. Married. A working woman in Bengaluru. You found out last week that your husband has been texting another woman from his office. He says it is nothing serious and has promised to stop. You are on a long phone call with your sister. You have decided to stay in the marriage. But in describing what happened you are directing almost all your anger at the other woman and almost none at your husband. You are telling your sister what kind of woman she must be to text a married man and what women like her want.
